medications.indication_rationale: Softens impacted wax before removal; source used docusate 0.5% then twice-daily drops for 5 days. Presupposes no TM perforation — confirm on otoscopy.
medications.dose_route_frequency: instil twice daily for ~5 days, then arrange removal (as per source note)
contraindications_in_this_case: Avoid if TM perforation suspected — confirm intact TM at in-person review

medications.drug_name: ear syringing/irrigation performed now (before softening / without TM check)
medications.indication_rationale: Irrigating hard impacted wax without softening or without confirming an intact TM risks pain, canal trauma and TM injury; defer to in-person after softening.

behavioural_change_actions.specific_action: Lie with the affected ear up for ~5 minutes after instilling drops so they pool against the wax; do NOT insert cotton buds.
behavioural_change_actions.rationale: Improves cerumenolytic penetration; cotton buds pack wax deeper against the TM.

patient_education_points.point: This looks like ear wax that swelled with water and blocked the canal — usually harmless and reversible. But because the hearing dropped suddenly, you need someone to look in your ear soon to be sure it's wax and not a nerve-related hearing loss, which needs urgent treatment.
Use the softening drops and never poke cotton buds in the ear. Your hearing may stay blocked (or briefly feel worse) until the softened wax is removed by a clinician.
Go to ED / call 000 for severe spinning dizziness, facial weakness, bleeding or foul discharge from the ear, or severe spreading pain behind the ear.

follow_up_plan.what_to_reassess: Confirm wax vs SSNHL; remove softened wax by irrigation/micro-suction; audiometry/ENT if canal clear.
follow_up_plan.trigger_for_earlier_review: Vertigo, facial weakness, discharge/bleeding, or no improvement/canal clear (possible SSNHL) — urgent ENT/ED.

must_include_items: Arrange prompt in-person otoscopy — do not assume sudden hearing loss is wax
Actively consider/exclude sudden sensorineural hearing loss (time-critical)
Cerumenolytic softening with no cotton buds
acceptable_alternatives: Any cerumenolytic (docusate, olive oil, sodium bicarbonate); micro-suction or irrigation for removal once TM confirmed intact
errors_of_commission: Reassuring it's 'just wax' with no examination arranged
Recommending immediate home syringing of hard wax / without TM check
scoring_rubric.passing_threshold_notes: Pass requires arranging prompt otoscopy AND explicitly considering SSNHL; assuming benign wax without examination is a critical reasoning failure.
